name: Humaans
h1: Build AI Agents That Work with Humaans
metaTitle: Build Humaans AI Agents with DocsBot Skill Builder
metaDescription: Create DocsBot AI agents that use Humaans for employee data lookups, onboarding workflows, approvals, and internal support actions with safe controls.
intro: Humaans can become part of a DocsBot AI agent that helps teams answer questions, retrieve employee information, and complete approved HR workflows. With DocsBot Skill Builder, you describe the outcome you want in plain language, and the builder designs, secures, tests, and packages the Skill for your support or internal agent. This helps teams move from static answers to AI Actions that look up records, prepare handoffs, update data, and trigger bounded workflows safely.
supportUseCases: Use a customer-facing DocsBot agent to verify employee-related account context in Humaans before preparing a secure support handoff for payroll, benefits, or access questions.
Let a helpdesk-connected DocsBot agent summarize relevant employee profile details from Humaans so support teams can respond faster to worker requests without switching systems.
Have the agent collect missing context from a requester and create an HR follow-up workflow tied to the right employee record in Humaans.
Enable the agent to check onboarding or offboarding status in Humaans and give customers or workers a clear next-step update during a support conversation.
Use Humaans data to help the agent prepare an escalation summary for HR or people operations when a request needs human review.
internalUseCases: Employees can chat with a private DocsBot agent in Slack, Microsoft Teams, or chat to look up approved Humaans information such as manager, team, start date, or onboarding status.
People operations teams can ask the agent to summarize employee record details from Humaans, gather missing context, and prepare the next HR workflow step.
Managers can chat with the agent to check onboarding progress, confirm reporting lines, or generate a handoff summary for internal approvals using Humaans data.
IT and operations employees can use the agent to coordinate joiner, mover, and leaver workflows by combining Humaans lookups with downstream task triggers and status summaries.
HR teams can chat with the agent to follow SOPs, retrieve employee information, and complete approved updates with clear approval boundaries when sensitive changes are involved.
whatYouCanBuild: An AI agent that looks up employee profiles and answers internal HR questions from Humaans.
A support agent that gathers worker context and prepares HR ticket handoffs using Humaans data.
An onboarding assistant that checks status, lists missing steps, and triggers approved follow-up workflows.
A manager assistant that retrieves team structure and summarizes employee details from Humaans records.
An offboarding workflow agent that confirms employee status and coordinates approved downstream actions.
An internal HR copilot that follows SOPs and drafts escalation summaries from Humaans information.

howItWorks.title: Describe the outcome
howItWorks.description: You tell Skill Builder what you want the Humaans-powered agent to do, such as answer employee questions, check onboarding status, or prepare HR handoffs, and it turns that goal into a practical Skill design.

howItWorks.title: Skill Builder designs it
howItWorks.description: Skill Builder decides how the agent should use Humaans, what information it should look up or update, which workflow steps need approvals, and how to keep actions within safe business limits.

howItWorks.title: Connections are secured
howItWorks.description: Skill Builder identifies the required configuration values, handles authentication safely, keeps credentials out of chats and Skill source, and proxies secrets through secure runtime configuration by default.

howItWorks.title: Access is carefully scoped
howItWorks.description: Skill Builder limits the Skill to the minimum records, actions, users, and systems needed for the workflow, and it adds approval boundaries wherever a sensitive HR action should require review.

howItWorks.title: Tests and improvements run
howItWorks.description: The automated testing agent runs imagined support and internal team scenarios against the Skill, finds bugs, weak instructions, or permission gaps, and feeds those results into a self-improvement loop before deployment.

faq.question: How can I build an AI agent that works with Humaans?
faq.answer: With DocsBot Skill Builder, you describe the Humaans workflow you want in plain language, and Skill Builder designs the Skill, secures the connection, tests likely scenarios, and prepares it for deployment to a support or internal agent.

faq.question: Can an AI support agent use Humaans data during customer or worker conversations?
faq.answer: Yes, a DocsBot support agent can use approved Humaans data to look up employee context, summarize status, gather missing details, and prepare handoffs or escalations while replying in chat or through helpdesk tickets.

faq.question: What internal team workflows can an AI assistant automate with Humaans?
faq.answer: An internal DocsBot agent can help with employee lookups, onboarding and offboarding checks, manager and team information requests, SOP guidance, status summaries, and approved workflow triggers across HR operations.

faq.question: Can DocsBot safely update records or trigger actions in Humaans?
faq.answer: DocsBot Skills can be designed for bounded actions such as approved updates, workflow triggers, or record-linked handoffs, with scoped permissions and approval boundaries set by Skill Builder for sensitive steps.

faq.question: How does DocsBot keep Humaans credentials and HR data secure?
faq.answer: Skill Builder defines secure configuration values, protects and proxies secrets through runtime controls, keeps credentials out of conversations and generated content, and scopes access to only the data and actions needed for the workflow.

faq.question: Can an employee AI assistant in Slack or Microsoft Teams use Humaans?
faq.answer: Yes, employees can chat with a private DocsBot agent in Slack, Teams, or a web chat interface to retrieve approved Humaans information, follow HR workflows, and get summaries without opening multiple systems.
